rawOutput:
{
  "topic": "Psoriasis Pathophysiology: A 10-Mark Answer",
  "topic_slug": "psoriasis-pathophysiology-10-mark-answer",
  "level": "Post-Graduate (Advanced)",
  "goal": "Post-graduate dermatology exam preparation (specifically structured for a 10-mark theory answer)",
  "estimated_total_minutes": 300,
  "modules": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "title": "Etiology: Genetic Susceptibility & Environmental Triggers",
      "estimated_minutes": 45,
      "description": "Explores the foundational causes of psoriasis, focusing on specific genetic loci and how environmental stressors act as primary catalysts. Essential for scoring the 'Etiology' marks in a PG exam.",
      "plan": {
        "goal": "Correlate specific genetic loci with environmental triggers to explain disease susceptibility.",
        "concepts": [
          "PSORS1 to PSORS9 loci",
          "HLA-Cw6 allele association",
          "IL23R and TNFAIP3 mutations",
          "Koebner phenomenon mechanisms",
          "Streptococcal pharyngitis trigger (superantigens)",
          "Drug-induced psoriasis (Lithium, Beta-blockers, NSAIDs, Antimalarials)"
        ],
        "sourceRefs": [
          "Chapter 31: Psoriasis"
        ],
        "prerequisites": []
      },
      "subtopics": [],
      "generated": false,
      "checkpoint": {
        "type": "quiz",
        "num_questions": 5,
        "pass_threshold": 80,
        "question_types": [
          "conceptual",
          "application",
          "true_false"
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "id": 2,
      "title": "The Initiation Phase: Innate Immune Activation",
      "estimated_minutes": 45,
      "description": "Details the immediate molecular response to epidermal stress. This module bridges the gap between a physical/environmental trigger and the complex immune cascade.",
      "plan": {
        "goal": "Map the molecular events from keratinocyte trauma to the activation of myeloid dendritic cells.",
        "concepts": [
          "Keratinocyte stress response",
          "Antimicrobial peptides (AMPs)",
          "LL-37 (Cathelicidin)",
          "Self-DNA/RNA and LL-37 complex formation",
          "Toll-like receptors (TLR7 and TLR9)",
          "Plasmacytoid Dendritic Cells (pDCs) activation",
          "Interferon-alpha (IFN-α) secretion",
          "Myeloid Dendritic Cell (mDC) recruitment"
        ],
        "sourceRefs": [
          "Chapter 31: Psoriasis"
        ],
        "prerequisites": []
      },
      "subtopics": [],
      "generated": false,
      "checkpoint": {
        "type": "quiz",
        "num_questions": 5,
        "pass_threshold": 80,
        "question_types": [
          "conceptual",
          "true_false"
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "id": 3,
      "title": "The Maintenance Phase: The IL-23/Th17 Axis",
      "estimated_minutes": 60,
      "description": "The immunological core of the disease. Mastery of this axis is critical for PG students, as it explains both the deep pathogenesis and the rationale for modern biologic therapies.",
      "plan": {
        "goal": "Diagram the IL-23/Th17 pathway as the central driver of psoriatic inflammation.",
        "concepts": [
          "mDC migration to lymph nodes",
          "IL-12 driven Th1 differentiation (TNF-α, IFN-γ)",
          "IL-23 as the master inflammatory regulator",
          "Th17 and Th22 cell survival and proliferation",
          "Effector functions of IL-17A and IL-17F",
          "Effector functions of IL-22",
          "Biologic therapeutic targets (TNF-α, IL-23, IL-17)"
        ],
        "sourceRefs": [
          "Chapter 31: Psoriasis"
        ],
        "prerequisites": [
          "Myeloid Dendritic Cell (mDC) recruitment"
        ]
      },
      "subtopics": [],
      "generated": false,
      "checkpoint": {
        "type": "quiz",
        "num_questions": 6,
        "pass_threshold": 80,
        "question_types": [
          "conceptual",
          "application"
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "id": 4,
      "title": "Keratinocyte Kinetics & The Feed-Forward Loop",
      "estimated_minutes": 45,
      "description": "Explains how keratinocytes shift from being 'victims' of inflammation to active drivers, creating the chronic, self-sustaining nature of psoriatic plaques.",
      "plan": {
        "goal": "Explain the self-perpetuating inflammatory circuit and the altered kinetics of keratinocyte proliferation.",
        "concepts": [
          "Reduction of keratinocyte cell cycle (28 days to 3-5 days)",
          "Keratinocytes as active immune drivers",
          "Chemokine release (CXCL1, CXCL8)",
          "Neutrophil and T-cell recruitment to the epidermis",
          "The 'Vicious Cycle' (feed-forward mechanism)",
          "Angiogenesis driven by TNF-α and IL-17"
        ],
        "sourceRefs": [
          "Chapter 31: Psoriasis"
        ],
        "prerequisites": [
          "Effector functions of IL-17A and IL-17F",
          "Effector functions of IL-22"
        ]
      },
      "subtopics": [],
      "generated": false,
      "checkpoint": {
        "type": "quiz",
        "num_questions": 4,
        "pass_threshold": 75,
        "question_types": [
          "conceptual",
          "true_false"
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "id": 5,
      "title": "Clinical-Histopathological Correlation",
      "estimated_minutes": 45,
      "description": "Translates the molecular and cellular changes into the microscopic and clinical signs required in dermatology exams.",
      "plan": {
        "goal": "Map specific molecular and cellular events directly to their resulting histological hallmarks and clinical signs.",
        "concepts": [
          "Acanthosis (epidermal thickening)",
          "Parakeratosis (nuclear retention in stratum corneum)",
          "Munro’s Microabscesses (neutrophil collections)",
          "Elongated Rete Ridges ('test-tube' appearance)",
          "Dilatation and tortuosity of dermal capillary loops",
          "Auspitz sign pathophysiology"
        ],
        "sourceRefs": [
          "Chapter 31: Psoriasis"
        ],
        "prerequisites": [
          "Reduction of keratinocyte cell cycle (28 days to 3-5 days)",
          "Neutrophil and T-cell recruitment to the epidermis",
          "Angiogenesis driven by TNF-α and IL-17"
        ]
      },
      "subtopics": [],
      "generated": false,
      "checkpoint": {
        "type": "quiz",
        "num_questions": 5,
        "pass_threshold": 80,
        "question_types": [
          "application",
          "conceptual"
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "id": 6,
      "title": "Capstone: Constructing the 10-Mark Exam Answer & Flowcharts",
      "estimated_minutes": 60,
      "description": "The final synthesis. Takes all previous modules and structures them into a highly reproducible, high-yield format designed specifically for the PG theory exam.",
      "plan": {
        "goal": "Synthesize the entire pathophysiology into a highly structured 10-mark theory answer complete with reproducible schematics/flowcharts.",
        "concepts": [
          "10-Mark Answer Structure (Definition, Etiology, Initiation, Maintenance, Cellular, Histology)",
          "Designing the 'Feed-Forward' Flowchart",
          "Designing the Innate-to-Adaptive Axis Flowchart",
          "Highlighting examiner keywords (HLA-Cw6, IL-23/Th17, Auspitz)",
          "Integrating biologic targets for 'extra marks'",
          "Time management for a 10-mark question"
        ],
        "sourceRefs": [
          "Chapter 31: Psoriasis"
        ],
        "prerequisites": [
          "PSORS1 to PSORS9 loci",
          "Self-DNA/RNA and LL-37 complex formation",
          "IL-23 as the master inflammatory regulator",
          "The 'Vicious Cycle' (feed-forward mechanism)",
          "Auspitz sign pathophysiology"
        ]
      },
      "subtopics": [],
      "generated": false,
      "checkpoint": {
        "type": "quiz",
        "num_questions": 3,
        "pass_threshold": 100,
        "question_types": [
          "application"
        ]
      }
    }
  ]
}

parsed.goal: Post-graduate dermatology exam preparation (specifically structured for a 10-mark theory answer)

modules.title: Etiology: Genetic Susceptibility & Environmental Triggers
modules.description: Explores the foundational causes of psoriasis, focusing on specific genetic loci and how environmental stressors act as primary catalysts. Essential for scoring the 'Etiology' marks in a PG exam.

plan.goal: Correlate specific genetic loci with environmental triggers to explain disease susceptibility.

modules.title: The Initiation Phase: Innate Immune Activation
modules.description: Details the immediate molecular response to epidermal stress. This module bridges the gap between a physical/environmental trigger and the complex immune cascade.

plan.goal: Map the molecular events from keratinocyte trauma to the activation of myeloid dendritic cells.

modules.title: The Maintenance Phase: The IL-23/Th17 Axis
modules.description: The immunological core of the disease. Mastery of this axis is critical for PG students, as it explains both the deep pathogenesis and the rationale for modern biologic therapies.

plan.goal: Diagram the IL-23/Th17 pathway as the central driver of psoriatic inflammation.
concepts: IL-23 as the master inflammatory regulator
Th17 and Th22 cell survival and proliferation
Effector functions of IL-17A and IL-17F

modules.title: Keratinocyte Kinetics & The Feed-Forward Loop
modules.description: Explains how keratinocytes shift from being 'victims' of inflammation to active drivers, creating the chronic, self-sustaining nature of psoriatic plaques.

plan.goal: Explain the self-perpetuating inflammatory circuit and the altered kinetics of keratinocyte proliferation.
concepts: Reduction of keratinocyte cell cycle (28 days to 3-5 days)
Neutrophil and T-cell recruitment to the epidermis
Angiogenesis driven by TNF-α and IL-17
prerequisites: Effector functions of IL-17A and IL-17F

modules.title: Clinical-Histopathological Correlation
modules.description: Translates the molecular and cellular changes into the microscopic and clinical signs required in dermatology exams.

plan.goal: Map specific molecular and cellular events directly to their resulting histological hallmarks and clinical signs.
concepts: Dilatation and tortuosity of dermal capillary loops
prerequisites: Reduction of keratinocyte cell cycle (28 days to 3-5 days)
Neutrophil and T-cell recruitment to the epidermis
Angiogenesis driven by TNF-α and IL-17

modules.title: Capstone: Constructing the 10-Mark Exam Answer & Flowcharts
modules.description: The final synthesis. Takes all previous modules and structures them into a highly reproducible, high-yield format designed specifically for the PG theory exam.

plan.goal: Synthesize the entire pathophysiology into a highly structured 10-mark theory answer complete with reproducible schematics/flowcharts.
concepts: 10-Mark Answer Structure (Definition, Etiology, Initiation, Maintenance, Cellular, Histology)
prerequisites: IL-23 as the master inflammatory regulator

modules.title: The Initiation Phase: Innate Immune Activation
The Maintenance Phase: The IL-23/Th17 Axis
